disc_01_desc: I work with just about every kind of data — video, audio, image, text, spatial and tabular — to understand how complex systems are structured and how they change over time.
disc_02_desc: I help organisations understand complex data. From messy raw datasets to clear insight, I design analyses and visualisations that reveal structure, highlight patterns, and communicate results in ways that are both rigorous and intuitive.
disc_03_desc: I build custom analytical tools when existing methods are not enough. My work combines machine learning, signal analysis, and advanced statistical modelling to extract meaningful patterns from large and noisy datasets.
disc_04_desc: Analysis only works if people understand it. I craft figures, maps, and interactive visuals that respect the nuance of the data while staying legible to researchers, funders, and the wider public.
gallery_item_1_detail: Consultancy work for the citizen science project BirdNET at Cornell University. This interactive system was developed for a visitor centre, allowing the public to explore both real-time and historical patterns in biodiversity data. The interface was designed to make large acoustic monitoring datasets accessible and intuitive.
gallery_item_2_detail: Between 2011 and 2016, I worked in documentary film production as a cinematographer and second unit director. This sequence, shot in Belarus, opens the trailer for Awaken, which screened in Times Square, New York. Over those years I led small international units across three continents, capturing large-format landscape and nature footage in often demanding conditions.
gallery_item_3_detail: I analyze and visualize complex data—from physical artifacts to large-scale statistical trends—to extract insights and create evidence-based narratives for both experts and the public. This example shows a stone tool from the African Early Stone Age.
gallery_item_3_credits: From the Fish Hoek Valley Museum collection · Scanned by Global Digital Heritage · CC BY-NC 4.0
gallery_item_4_desc: From aerial imagery to interactive mapping
gallery_item_4_detail: I built a machine learning pipeline to detect and map individual tree species from aerial photography — then turned the results into an interactive browser tool anyone can explore. Users can slide between raw imagery and segmentation overlays, and inspect species-specific data in real time. Part of a UKRI-funded project at the University of Oxford.
gallery_item_5_detail: I hold a PhD in Biology from the University of Oxford, where I went on to work as a postdoctoral researcher and remain a visiting researcher. My research on ecology, bioacoustics, and cultural evolution has been published in journals including Current Biology and Methods in Ecology and Evolution, and covered by international media.
gallery_item_6_detail: As a Senior Conservation Scientist at the RSPB, I work on the Big Garden Birdwatch — one of the world's largest citizen science projects, engaging half a million participants each year. I develop statistical methods and data pipelines to turn millions of public observations into meaningful trends, helping to track how common garden birds are faring across the UK.
bio_body: My work sits between research, computation, and visual communication. I have been a researcher at the University of Oxford and Senior Conservation Scientist at Europe's largest nature conservation charity. Earlier in my career I worked in film, shooting projects around the world. Today, I design analytical tools and visualizations that help researchers and organisations understand complex data and communicate it clearly. I run
bio_studio_suffix: , a small studio at the intersection of data science and design.
contact_cta: Available for consulting and collaborations. Reach out if you have an interesting project or just want to say hi!
